title: Dependency Security Delta Review
description: Review package and lockfile changes for executable supply-chain risk before accepting them.
body: Inputs: manifest diff, lockfile diff, package manager output, and advisory data when available. Use when dependencies, registries, build images, or plugin sources change. Do not make legal license conclusions. Procedure: list direct additions, large version jumps, new install scripts, binary downloads, registry changes, and high-severity advisories; run available tests without new network surprises. Output: accept, require human review, or reject with evidence. Human gate: unpinned sources, new install-time code, or abandoned security-critical package. Use when Security Risk Controls work touches trust boundaries, authorization decisions, dangerous sinks, secrets, and dependency execution. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: run the documented inspection and validation workflow; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include negative security tests, server-side checks, sink payload tests, and secret scans. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves client-only controls, injection, credential leakage, permissive fallback, and unsafe dependency changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
